title: Deterministic Infra for Non-Deterministic AI Agents - Nishant Gupta, Meta Superintelligence Labs
summary: Nishant Gupta from Meta discusses infrastructure design for autonomous AI agents that operate reliably in production environments. The talk addresses the gap between deterministic microservice infrastructure and non-deterministic LLM-based agents, covering orchestration, observability, retry handling, safety guardrails, and failure recovery patterns.
writeup: Nishant Gupta of Meta Superintelligence Labs examines how to build infrastructure that runs non-deterministic LLM-based agents reliably in production, where traditional microservice infrastructure assumes deterministic behavior. The talk covers orchestration, observability, retry handling, safety guardrails, and failure recovery patterns. It addresses concrete design concerns including session isolation, elastic compute, the control plane, and error handling. The framing matters because autonomous agents introduce variability that conventional deterministic infrastructure was not built to absorb.
